      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • calendarFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder calendarFormat(CalendarFormat value)
      Calendar wire format.
      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • dateFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder dateFormat(DateFormat value)
      Date wire format.
      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • temporalFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder temporalFormat(TemporalFormat value)
      Temporal wire format.
      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • timeZoneFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder timeZoneFormat(TimeZoneFormat value)
      Time-zone wire format.
      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • localeFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder localeFormat(LocaleFormat value)
      Locale wire format.
      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • binaryFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder binaryFormat(BinaryFormat value)
      Binary wire format.

      Controls how byte[] values are written to and read from text-based serializer / parser wire formats, and the encoding used by stream-based serializers when converting their byte output to a string.

      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • enumFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder enumFormat(EnumFormat value)
      Enum wire format.
      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • uuidFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder uuidFormat(UuidFormat value)
      UUID wire format.

      Controls how UUID values are written to text-based wire formats. Binary serializers (BSON / CBOR / MsgPack / Prototext) emit native 16-byte binary regardless of this setting.

      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • bitSetFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder bitSetFormat(BitSetFormat value)
      BitSet wire format.

      Controls how BitSet values are written to text-based wire formats.

      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • bigNumberFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder bigNumberFormat(BigNumberFormat value)
      Big-number wire format.

      Controls how BigInteger and BigDecimal values are written to text-based wire formats. Useful for safe JS interop where values outside ±(2^53 − 1) would silently lose precision.

      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • booleanFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder booleanFormat(BooleanFormat value)
      Boolean wire format.

      Controls how Boolean / boolean values are written to text-based wire formats. Binary serializers (BSON / CBOR / MsgPack / Prototext / Parquet) emit a native boolean wire type regardless of this setting.

      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • floatFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder floatFormat(FloatFormat value)
      Float / Double non-finite wire format.

      Controls how non-finite Float / Double values (NaN / ±Infinity) are written to text-based wire formats. The default FloatFormat.NaN_AS_NULL is JSON-spec-compliant. Binary serializers emit native IEEE-754 floats regardless of this setting.

      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • currencyFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder currencyFormat(CurrencyFormat value)
      Currency wire format.

      Controls how Currency values are written to text-based wire formats. The default CurrencyFormat.ISO_CODE is the only setting safe for machine-to-machine wires; CurrencyFormat.SYMBOL and CurrencyFormat.DISPLAY_NAME are locale-sensitive and best-effort on the parse side.

      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • classFormat

      public MarshallingContext.Builder classFormat(ClassFormat value)
      Class wire format.

      Controls how Class values are written to text-based wire formats. The default ClassFormat.FQCN matches the historical ClassSwap behavior. ClassFormat.SIMPLE_NAME is serialize-only — the parser path throws UnsupportedOperationException.

      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • classLoader

      public MarshallingContext.Builder classLoader(ClassLoader value)
      Session classloader.

      Sets the classloader used when parsing wire-format class names (e.g. FQCN or binary-name strings written by ClassFormatSwap). When null, the thread-context classloader is used as a fallback — which is the historical behavior.

      Useful in OSGi bundles, webapp classloaders, and plugin systems where the thread-context classloader does not have visibility into the classes that the session needs to resolve.

      Parameters:
      value - The classloader to use for class resolution. Can be null to reset to thread-context fallback.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • type

      public MarshallingContext.Builder type(Class<? extends Context> value)
      Description copied from class: Context.Builder
      Associates a context class with this builder.

      This is the type of object that this builder creates when the Context.Builder.build() method is called.

      By default, it's the outer class of where the builder class is defined.

      If null is set, Context.Builder.build() will throw an exception. The default constructor automatically sets this to the outer class, so null should only be set explicitly if you want to override the default.

      Overrides:
      type in class Context.Builder<MarshallingContext.Builder>
      Parameters:
      value - The context class that this builder should create.
      Can be null (will cause Context.Builder.build() to throw an exception).
      Returns:
      This object.

    • typeName

      public MarshallingContext.Builder typeName(Class<?> on, String value)
      An identifying name for this class.

      The name is used to identify the class type during parsing when it cannot be inferred through reflection. For example, if a bean property is of type Object, then the serializer will add the name to the output so that the class can be determined during parsing.

      It is also used to specify element names in XML.

      Example:

      // Use _type='mybean' to identify this bean. public class MyBean {...} // Create a serializer and specify the type name.. WriterSerializer serializer = JsonSerializer .create() .typeName(MyBean.class, "mybean") .build(); // Produces: {"_type":"mybean",...} String json = serializer.serialize(new MyBean());

      Notes:
      • Equivalent to the @Marshalled(typeName) annotation.
      See Also:
      • @Marshalled(typeName)
      • beanDictionary(Class...)
      Parameters:
      on - The class the type name is being defined on.
      Cannot be null.
      value - The new value for this setting.
      Cannot be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • typePropertyName

      public MarshallingContext.Builder typePropertyName(Class<?> on, String value)
      Bean type property name.

      Same as typePropertyName(String) except targets a specific bean class instead of globally.

      Example:

      // POJOs with @Marshalled(name) annotations. @Marshalled(typeName="foo") public class Foo {...} @Marshalled(typeName="bar") public class Bar {...} // A bean with a field with an indeterminate type. public class MyBean { public Object mySimpleField; } // Create a serializer that uses 't' instead of '_type' for dictionary names. WriterSerializer serializer = JsonSerializer .create() .typePropertyName(MyBean.class, "t") .dictionary(Foo.class, Bar.class) .build(); // Produces "{mySimpleField:{t:'foo',...}}". String json = serializer.serialize(new MyBean());

      See Also:
      • @Marshalled(typePropertyName)
      Parameters:
      on - The class the type property name applies to.
      Cannot be null.
      value - The new value for this setting.
      The default is "_type".
      Cannot be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • typePropertyName

      public MarshallingContext.Builder typePropertyName(String value)
      Bean type property name.

      This specifies the name of the bean property used to store the dictionary name of a bean type so that the parser knows the data type to reconstruct.

      Example:

      // POJOs with @Marshalled(name) annotations. @Marshalled(typeName="foo") public class Foo {...} @Marshalled(typeName="bar") public class Bar {...} // Create a serializer that uses 't' instead of '_type' for dictionary names. WriterSerializer serializer = JsonSerializer .create() .typePropertyName("t") .dictionary(Foo.class, Bar.class) .build(); // Create a serializer that uses 't' instead of '_type' for dictionary names. ReaderParser parser = JsonParser .create() .typePropertyName("t") .dictionary(Foo.class, Bar.class) .build(); // A bean with a field with an indeterminate type. public class MyBean { public Object mySimpleField; } // Produces "{mySimpleField:{t:'foo',...}}". String json = serializer.serialize(new MyBean()); // Parse bean. MyBean bean = parser.parse(json, MyBean.class);

      See Also:
      • Marshalled.typePropertyName()
      • MarshalledConfig.typePropertyName()
      Parameters:
      value - The new value for this setting.
      The default is "_type".
      Cannot be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • useJavaBeanIntrospector

      public MarshallingContext.Builder useJavaBeanIntrospector()
      Use Java Introspector.

      Using the built-in Java bean introspector will not pick up fields or non-standard getters/setters.
      Most @Marshalled annotations will be ignored.

      Example:

      // Create a serializer that only uses the built-in java bean introspector for finding properties. WriterSerializer serializer = JsonSerializer .create() .useJavaBeanIntrospector() .build();

      Returns:
      This object.

    • useJavaBeanIntrospector

      public MarshallingContext.Builder useJavaBeanIntrospector(boolean value)
      Same as useJavaBeanIntrospector() but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
      Parameters:
      value - The value for this setting.
      Returns:
      This object.
